tool_input.summary: PPI does not appear to host a standalone Saturn plasmoid/reconnection *event catalog* dataset (directory-name searches for 'plasmoid', 'reconnection', 'catalog', 'tail' returned no matches). To evaluate whether plasmoid-driven mass loss can balance Enceladus mass loading, you can combine Cassini MAG calibrated magnetic-field data (to identify reconnection/plasmoid signatures and timing) with Cassini plasma density/ion-moment products (to estimate mass content and flux).

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated Full Res. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Primary dataset for identifying Saturn magnetotail reconnection/plasmoid signatures (e.g., Bz reversals, dipolarizations, flux rope-like rotations) at the highest available cadence in Saturn-centric KRTP coordinates during the Saturn tour.

candidates.title: Cassini MAG Calibrated 1 Sec. Avg. Data in KRTP Coords. Collection
candidates.reasoning: Useful for event surveys/catalog building with manageable volume; still high enough cadence for many reconnection/plasmoid interval identifications.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Ion Moments Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Provides derived ion density/velocity/temperature moments (where available), enabling estimates of plasma mass density and flow during/around plasmoid intervals for mass-loss calculations.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Electron density proxy to constrain plasma density in regions/times when CAPS moments are unavailable or to cross-check; can be combined with composition assumptions to estimate mass density in plasmoids.

candidates.title: Cassini CAPS Fitted Parameters Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Forward-model fit products from CAPS plasma data (fits of distributions/parameters) that can supplement moments for density/temperature estimates relevant to mass-content estimates in reconnection outflow/plasmoids.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
This node has NO mission layer. ~767 datasets sit directly under data/.
Do NOT call pds_list_missions — it will return an empty list.
Instead, go directly to pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') using mission or instrument keywords to narrow the listing.
Then probe the most relevant directories with pds_probe_datasets.


Common mission/instrument keywords:
  Cassini → cassini (instruments: CAPS, MAG, MIMI, RPWS, UVIS, INMS, CDA, RADAR)
  Galileo → galileo (instruments: EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR)
  Juno → juno (instruments: Waves, JADE, JEDI, MAG, MWR, UVS)
  Voyager 1/2 → voyager1, voyager2 (instruments: CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS)
  New Horizons → new-horizons (instruments: PEPSSI, SWAP, SDC)
  Pioneer 10/11 → pioneer10, pioneer11 (instruments: CPI, GTT, MAG, PA)
  MESSENGER → messenger (instruments: EPPS, MAG, NS)
  Ulysses → ulysses (instruments: SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, SWICS, COSPIN)
  MAVEN → maven (instruments: MAG, NGIMS, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, LPW)
  THEMIS → themis (instruments: ESA, SST, FGM, SCM, EFI)

Dataset naming: directories often follow the pattern '<mission>-<target>-<instrument>-<description>' (e.g. 'cassini-caps-calibrated').
Use the filter parameter on list_dataset_dirs to search by mission or instrument name.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<keyword>') to find datasets.
  Step 2: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths.
  Step 3: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections.
  Step 4: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
Is the mass loss inferred from the detected Saturn plasmoid/reconnection event catalog sufficient to balance Enceladus as a plasma source?

Identification_Area.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Dougherty, Michele K.; Kellock, Stephen; Slootweg, A. Peter; Achilleos, Nicholas; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes
                1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn,       
                Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and 
                RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, 
                Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and 
                IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute 
                averaged data is provided as well.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Waite, J.H.; Furman, J.D.
Citation_Information.description:
The uncalibrated data were acquired in a mix of CAPS operating modes     
                beginning with the first instrument checkout in January 1999 and         
                containing throughout the Cassini Tour and through the end of prime      
                mission.  The data cover the time period from 1999-004T03:07:47     
                UT until end of prime mission (July 2008). In addition, it covers   
                data received during extended missions.  Sampling rates were variable    
                and depended upon the downlink capabilities and other activities         
                on-board.

results.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Identification_Area.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Citation_Information.author_list: Persoon, A.M.; Groene, J.B.; West, E.E.; Kurth, W.S.; Wahlund, J.-E.; Gurnett, D.A.
Citation_Information.description: This bundle provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies.  When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density.  At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.

Identification_Area.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies

Citation_Information.author_list: Persoon, A.M.; Groene, J.B.; West, E.E.; Kurth, W.S.; Wahlund, J.-E.; Gurnett, D.A.
Citation_Information.description: This collection provides electron number density values derived from features observed in plasma wave data obtained by the Cassini Radio and Plasma Wave Science (RPWS) instruments, along with observed or derived characteristic frequencies, and useful positional parameters for the spacecraft and related bodies.  When present, frequency values of narrowband emissions at the upper hybrid resonance were digitized and combined with measured or model magnetic field to derive electron number density.  At other times, features such as the upper cutoff in auroral hiss or electron plasma oscillations were used to determine the plasma frequency and electron density.

results.title: CASSINI ORBITER MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED AVERAGE DATA

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: CASSINI ORBITER MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED AVERAGE DATA
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: CASSINI MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED 1 SECOND AVERAGED ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED Cassini Orbiter Magnetometer Calibrated MAG data in 1 second averages. New versions (PRODUCT_VERSION_ID 6) of these data products, processed using an updated calibration, are in the process of being released in this dataset. Currently the data from 2001-01-01 through 2017-09-12 are version 6 products. Prior to this interval the products use an older calibration. The data are provided in RTN coordinates throughout the mission, with Earth, Jupiter, and Saturn centered coordinates for the respective flybys of those planets. These data have passed PDS peer review. Every effort has been made to ensure that the data and documentation are of the best possible quality. However, users of this data set are encouraged to verify the correctness of the data prior to submitting any publications or other work.

results.title: CASSINI ORBITER MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED FULL RES

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: CASSINI ORBITER MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED FULL RES
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: CASSINI MAGNETOMETER CALIBRATED FULL RES ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED Cassini Orbiter Magnetometer Calibrated MAG RDRs at the highest time resolution available covering the period 1998-12-30 (DOY 364) to 2017-09-15 (DOY 258). New versions (PRODUCT_VERSION_ID 5) of these data products, processed using an updated calibration, are in the process of being released. Currently the data from 2001-01-01 through 2017-09-15 are version 5 products. Prior to this interval the products use an older calibration. The data are in RTN coordinates prior Cassini's arrival at Saturn, and Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates at Saturn (beginning 2004-05-14, DOY 135). These data have passed PDS peer review. Every effort has been made to ensure that the data and documentation are of the best possible quality. However, users of this data set are encouraged to verify the correctness of the data prior to submitting any publications or other work.

CERTIFIED - This volume contains data and supporting documentation from the Cassini Orbiter CAPS derived ion moments data set, covering 2004-180 to 2012-154. These data have completed PDS peer review and are certified.
